title: Total Distance Traveled
query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(JumpDist), 0) AS total, COUNT(*) AS jumps FROM FSDJump WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {total} light year(s) jumped across {jumps} FSD jump(s) during the report period. The cosmic odometer — total light years is the single most evocative number a pilot can show; a weekly total spanning hundreds or thousands of ly is a genuine testament to how much of the galaxy you've moved through.
details.help: FSDJump JumpDist is the distance per jump in light years. total drives the title as the headline distance figure. summaryOnly = true because per-system reports always show 1 jump.

tagLines: FSD jump distance telemetry compiled. Total light years traveled archived in the navigation record.
Light years traveled. Not a metaphor. An actual distance, measured against the nearest star.
The galaxy is 100,000 light years across. This period you crossed a fraction of it. That fraction adds up.
